project_privacy_data_flows.data_type: Voice Audio for Wake Word Detection (Biometric/Sensitive)
project_privacy_data_flows.description: The app continuously captures microphone audio in the background and processes it through an on-device open-source microWakeWord TFLite model for local wake word detection. All audio processing is on-device; no audio is uploaded unless wake word is detected.

flow_steps.code: @RequiresPermission(Manifest.permission.RECORD_AUDIO) suspend fun start(coroutineScope, modelConfig, coroutineContext) { microWakeWordFactory(modelConfig).use { onListenerReady(modelConfig); runDetectionLoop(modelConfig, microWakeWord) } }
flow_steps.details: When the user enables wake word, the WakeWordListener loads the microWakeWord TFLite model and begins continuous listening.

flow_steps.code: private suspend fun runDetectionLoop(modelConfig: MicroWakeWordModelConfig, microWakeWord: MicroWakeWord) { while (isActive) { audioData = voiceAudioRecorder.audioData.receive(); microWakeWord.detectWakeWord(audioData) } }
flow_steps.details: Continuously receives audio chunks (~10ms each) from VoiceAudioRecorder and passes them through the local TFLite model for wake word detection. All processing is on-device.
When a wake word is detected, the callback triggers the Assist voice pipeline, which then starts streaming audio to the server.
